metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4009
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "TMEM16F / Anoctamin-6 could be a post-synaptic component of C-boutons"

Several experiments in Fig 2 show that TMEM16F protein is an alfa-motoneuron marker, which faces cholinergic C-boutons synapses.
- "By staining TMEM16F was observed in choline acetyltransferase-positive (ChAT+) motoneurons, specifically located in the ventral horn laminae VIII and IX. This protein can be observed forming clusters at the plasma membrane of soma and dendrites (Figs. 2A and B)"
- "Co-staining of TMEM16F with a plasma membrane marker of motoneurons (Na+/K+-ATPase alfa1) is shown in (Fig. 2F) verifying its localization.
In Fig 2G, TMEM16F protein is observed on the opposite side to the presynaptic marker Synaptophysin, which could indicate a postsynaptic localization, although strong evidence would be necessary.
Moreover, TMEM16F clusters are found facing the vesicular acetylcholine transporter (VAChT) pre-synaptic clusters (Fig. 2I), being VAChT a marker of synaptic cholinergic C-boutons.. Experimental description: 2-month-old wild-type C57BL/6J mice
